description:
You are an infrastructure engineer preparing a tiny
        “one-click” container bootstrap for a colleague.

        1. Create a new directory
           /home/user/provisioning
           (create any missing parent directory as well).

        2. Inside that directory write a **Bash script**
           named                  /home/user/provisioning/quick_provision.sh

           The script must contain **exactly three** Podman commands,
           in the exact order shown below (no comments, no blank lines,
           no additional commands).

           ------------------------------------------------------------
           podman pull docker.io/library/nginx:1.25-alpine
           podman run -d --name webtest -p 8080:80 docker.io/library/nginx:1.25-alpine
           podman ps --filter name=webtest
           ------------------------------------------------------------

           • Make the script executable ( chmod + x ).
           • Do **not** execute the script yourself; just create it.

        3. Also create a **log-template file**
           /home/user/provisioning/expected_output.log

           This file will show your colleague what the
           three commands are *expected* to print.
           The format must be **exactly** as follows, six lines total,
           with no leading or trailing spaces:

           ------------------------------------------------------------
           [STEP 1] Attempting to pull image docker.io/library/nginx:1.25-alpine
           [STEP 1] Image pulled successfully
           [STEP 2] Container webtest started with ID: CONTAINER_ID_PLACEHOLDER
           [STEP 2] Port 8080 mapped to container port 80
           [STEP 3] NAME=webtest STATUS=running IMAGE=docker.io/library/nginx:1.25-alpine
           [STEP 3] Command complete
           ------------------------------------------------------------

           • The string “CONTAINER_ID_PLACEHOLDER” must remain
             literally as-is; nothing else may appear on that line.
           • Every line must end with a single UNIX newline ( \n ).

        The automated tests will check:

        • that /home/user/provisioning exists;
        • that quick_provision.sh exists, is executable, **and its
          contents match exactly** the three lines shown above;
        • that expected_output.log exists **and matches exactly**
          the six-line template (including the placeholder text and
          newlines).

        Do not run the script; just create the directory, the script,
        set its permissions, and create the log-template file in the
        required format.
truth:
The verifier will look for:
        1. Directory /home/user/provisioning with default user-writable
           permissions (0700–0755 acceptable).

        2. File /home/user/provisioning/quick_provision.sh
           a) Permissions: executable by the file owner
              (any mode ≥0700 and ≤0755 accepted).
           b) Exact byte-for-byte content:
              Line 1: "podman pull docker.io/library/nginx:1.25-alpine\n"
              Line 2: "podman run -d --name webtest -p 8080:80 docker.io/library/nginx:1.25-alpine\n"
              Line 3: "podman ps --filter name=webtest\n"

        3. File /home/user/provisioning/expected_output.log
           Permissions: regular readable file (0644 acceptable).
           Exact byte-for-byte content (including the final newline):
              "[STEP 1] Attempting to pull image docker.io/library/nginx:1.25-alpine\n"
              "[STEP 1] Image pulled successfully\n"
              "[STEP 2] Container webtest started with ID: CONTAINER_ID_PLACEHOLDER\n"
              "[STEP 2] Port 8080 mapped to container port 80\n"
              "[STEP 3] NAME=webtest STATUS=running IMAGE=docker.io/library/nginx:1.25-alpine\n"
              "[STEP 3] Command complete\n"
name: task_000000_880bea52
